To what extent have IKEAâs sustainable practices affected its triple

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Research question is clearly stated, sharply focused and consistently drives the structure of the investigation
Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with comprehensive explanation of context and key terms
Methodology employs an appropriate range of relevant sources and methods with clear evidence of informed selection
weaknesses: Occasional descriptive detours (e.g. lengthy SWOT/STEEPLE explanations) momentarily distract from the research question
The title remains somewhat vague and could more precisely reflect the triple-bottom-line focus
Acknowledgement of bias in self-reported data is sound but concrete mitigation strategies are not fully operationalised
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although there are episodic lapses of focus and some descriptive redundancy, the candidate consistently delivers a well-focused, coherent investigation. The research question is explicit, arguable and tightly connected to each section of the report, and the methodology is appropriately varied and justified. These strengths align with the top of Band 5â6, meriting the maximum 6 marks.

strengths: Business-management terminology and concepts (CSR, TBL, ROCE, etc.) are used accurately and consistently throughout
Source material is generally well integrated, frequently converted into original tables and graphs to support argument development
Subject knowledge is clear and coherent, with effective linking of theory (e.g. Kotterâs model) to IKEAâs sustainability practices
weaknesses: A few data points (e.g. market-share figures, NPS values) lack precise citations, weakening credibility
Minor conceptual inaccuracies surface (e.g. confusion between margin and markup in one section)
Some tool expositions (SWOT, STEEPLE) remain descriptive rather than conceptually linked to the research question
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation demonstrates a high level of subject knowledge, effective and mostly appropriate application of source material, and accurate use of terminology. While not flawlessâoccasional citation gaps and descriptive excess occurâthe overall performance exceeds Band 3â4 and justifies a Band 5 mark for Knowledge and Understanding.

strengths: Analysis exhibits depth through multi-faceted insights linking financial trends to CSR and stakeholder theory
Arguments are well structured and conclusions are generally well supported by qualitative and quantitative evidence
Research is broadly relevant and demonstrates clear linkage to the research question across social, environmental and financial dimensions
weaknesses: Heavy reliance on IKEAâs self-reported data limits independent validation and occasional causal inferences are asserted without rigorous testing
Critical evaluation of some impacts (e.g. supplier cost burdens, philanthropic responsibilities) remains superficial
Anecdotal evidence (e.g. ex-employee quotes) is insufficiently assessed for reliability and context
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work falls solidly within the top Band 7â9 for Critical Thinking: it demonstrates good research quality, clear analysis, and well-developed argumentation. Despite several areas where evaluation could be more critical or data more rigorously triangulated, the overall sophistication and coherence justify a high-end Band 7â9 score of 9.

strengths: Report structure is comprehensive and logically sequenced with all required elements (title page, TOC, headings)
Formatting conventions are applied correctly (consistent font, numbered pages, clear captions for visuals)
Visuals (tables, charts, diagrams) are well integrated and enhance readability and comprehension
weaknesses: A few figure labels and axis titles are truncated or lack unit specification
Occasional grammatical errors and fragmented sentences detract slightly from professionalism
Some lengthy excerpts (e.g. full standards text) could be relocated to appendices to improve concision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is consistently strong, meeting all IB structural and formatting requirements. Minor cosmetic issues do not detract from clarity or coherence, placing the work firmly at the top of Band 3â4 and warranting the full 4 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The research questionââTo what extent have IKEAâs sustainable practices affected its triple bottom line?ââis explicit, arguable and focused on measurable social, environmental and financial outcomes. Subsequent sections (People, Planet, Profit) map directly onto the question, demonstrating a tight connection between RQ and discussion.  

Scope is manageable (2012-2023, specific initiatives such as People & Planet Positive) and key terms are operationalised. Occasional drift occurs when lengthy tool descriptions (SWOT, STEEPLE) are presented without always linking back to the RQ, but these moments do not undermine the overall focus.  

Because the question is clearly formulated, well delimited and consistently revisited, performance aligns with Band 5-6; a score of 5 is appropriate rather than 6 due to some episodic loss of sharp focus.

The essay advances a balanced discussion of benefits and drawbacks for each stakeholder group and environmental metric, frequently acknowledging limitations (e.g., bias in self-reported data, negative labour implications). The conclusion synthesises findings and revisits the research question.  

Critical evaluation, however, varies in depth; some sections (e.g., supplier cost burdens) stop at description without exploring strategic trade-offs or alternative interpretations. Recommendations or reflections on future prospects are brief and not strongly justified.  

Accordingly, the argument is âwell-developedâ with âpartially critical evaluationâ, matching Band 7-9; a score of 7 is appropriate, just below the mid-band due to uneven critical rigour.

The essay demonstrates clear understanding of CSR theory, triple-bottom-line, Kotterâs change model and financial ratio analysis, applying them meaningfully to IKEAâs context. Explanations of each concept precede use, and linkages between theory and data (e.g., TBL sections) show coherent subject knowledge.  

Nevertheless, occasional inaccuracies surface (e.g., conflating profit margin trends with absolute profitability; minor mis-labelling of CSR levels). Some analyses remain descriptive rather than conceptual, and overlong tool expositions detract from depth.  

Knowledge is therefore âclear and coherentâ but falls short of the consistent sophistication required for Band 5-6, justifying a top-of-Band 3-4 score of 4.

Business-management terminologyâCSR, TBL, ROCE, current ratio, IWAY, FSC, Kotterâs change, etc.âis used accurately in most instances and woven smoothly into the narrative. Definitions are provided for key terms, and acronyms are generally clarified.  

Minor lapses appear (e.g., occasional grammatical errors; slight confusion between margin and markup; inconsistent use of percentage formats) but do not obscure meaning.  

The overall command of concepts is âmostly accurate and consistentâ, matching Band 3-4 expectations; the stronger end of the band (4) is awarded.

Analysis integrates financial trend evaluation with CSR frameworks and stakeholder theory, yielding **clear, multi-faceted insights** (e.g., linking renewable-energy investment to margin trends and stakeholder perceptions). Decomposition of ratio movements and mapping of benefits/downsides across People, Planet, Profit show depth beyond description.  

However, some causal claims (e.g., website visits driving revenue, COâ cuts boosting profit) are asserted without rigorous statistical testing; data visualisations are occasionally taken at face value. These gaps limit the sophistication and originality expected for 10-12.  

Thus, the work fulfills âclear analysis with well-supported conclusionsâ characteristic of Band 7-9; an 8 reflects its upper-band quality with identifiableâbut not pervasiveâlimitations.

Source materialâfinancial statements, sustainability reports, Statista datasets, and scholarly articlesâis integrated through numerous charts and in-text commentary. The majority of citations are relevant to the RQ, and data are converted into original tables/graphs that support argument development.  

However, some figures (e.g., in-store customer numbers, certain survey percentages) lack clear referencing or derive from secondary web articles of uncertain rigour. A few graphs appear to replicate headline statistics without critical interrogation of methodology. These lapses slightly weaken appropriateness and credibility.  

Given the largely sound but not flawless application, the strand attains the top of Band 3-4 with a score of 4.

The student employs a wide chronological data set (2012-2023) and multiple methods: ratio analysis, benchmarking, CSR and change-management frameworks, SWOT/STEEPLE and document analysis of CSR and annual reports. This represents a generally relevant and varied methodological mix.  

Evidence of informed selection is uneven. Heavy dependence on IKEAâs self-reported figures is acknowledged, yet concrete triangulation (e.g., third-party carbon-audit data, peer-reviewed financial analyses) is limited. Data-collection and reliability sections show awareness of bias, but mitigation strategies remain largely descriptive rather than operationalised.  

Overall, the range and relevance merit Band 3-4; the upper mark of 4 recognises breadth and partial justification while reflecting shortcomings in source balance and methodological rigour.

The investigation draws on an extensive set of quantitative (financial ratios, COâ metrics, web-traffic data) and qualitative sources (reports, stakeholder testimonials), all directly linked to the RQ. Selection shows clear relevance and supports a balanced consideration of social, environmental and financial effects.  

Yet, the research base is skewed toward IKEAâs own disclosures, and the student seldom validates these figures through independent industry or academic studies. This constraint prevents the work from reaching the âconsistently excellentâ standard.  

Consequently, the strand sits in Band 7-9 for âgood research clearly relevantâ; the lower mark of 7 recognises breadth while accounting for the reliance on self-reported data.

Formatting conventionsâconsistent font, heading hierarchy, page numbers, tables/figures with captions, and a comprehensive reference listâare evident. Graphs are generally legible and placed near related text, enhancing readability.  

Minor issues persist: some axis labels are truncated, certain figure captions lack analytical commentary, and cell spacing in the SWOT table is uneven. Nonetheless, these lapses are cosmetic rather than substantive, and overall presentation meets IB formal requirements.  

Consequently, the strand attains the top band (3-4) for correct application of layout and formatting conventions; a score of 4 is justified.

The introduction states the organisational context, defines sustainability and CSR, and justifies the significance of analysing IKEAâs triple-bottom-line performance. Key terms such as âPeople & Planet Positiveâ and âtriple bottom lineâ are unpacked and situated historically, giving the reader a solid grasp of what will be explored.  

Minor weaknesses remain: the title is vague (âIKEAâs contribution to their sustainable goalsâ) and repeats the brandâs name inaccurately; some background details (e.g. furniture-industry COâ figures) are descriptive rather than explanatory. Nevertheless, the overall communication of topic and its parameters is accurate, focused and sufficiently thorough.  

Given the overall clarity and breadth of explanation, but noting occasional redundancy and uneven depth, the strand fits securely in Band 5-6; a mid-band score of 5 is awarded.

The report contains all required structural elementsâtitle page with word count, abstract-style introduction, table of contents, logically ordered headings (methodology, analysis by TBL dimensions, conclusion, references). Visuals are clearly numbered and integrated. Cross-referencing between sections is generally effective, guiding the reader through complex material.  

Isolated redundancies (e.g., repeated explanation of the TBL, lengthy tool descriptions) affect concision but not structural appropriateness. Overall, the structure fulfils expectations of clarity and coherence.  

Therefore, the work achieves Band 3-4 performance; the higher mark of 4 reflects completeness and effective sequencing.

annotations.comment: The word count is provided at the start of the report, satisfying the IB formatting requirement.
The table of contents is comprehensive and matches the reportâs major sections, enhancing navigability.
Employees and suppliers are introduced abruptly without a subheading. Adding clear subheadings here would improve structural coherence.
The formaldehyde reduction analysis effectively links health impacts to customer well-being, demonstrating clear subject knowledge and relevance to the triple-bottom-line question.

The title âIKEAâs contribution to their sustainable goalsâ is too vague. A more precise title could reflect the focus on measurable social, environmental and financial outcomes under the triple-bottom-line framework.
The discussion of data reliability acknowledges bias but stops short of operationalising mitigation. Specify concrete steps (e.g., cross-referencing carbon audits) to reduce bias.
SWOT table presents a â6.5% market shareâ figure without a clear source citation. Ensure each data point includes a precise reference to maintain credibility.
The Opportunities entry âpeople can paid to work thereâ contains grammatical errors and unclear phrasing. Revise language for clarity and professionalism.
Shareholder performance data is drawn entirely from IKEA sources. To strengthen analysis, corroborate dividend trends with independent financial analysis.
The website visits graph shows a post-2021 decline but the text does not analyse this reversal. Discuss possible reasons for this downturn for deeper insight.
NPS is mentioned but values and time periods are not provided here. Include specific scores and dates to support the claim of improved customer loyalty.
IWAY compliance rates are reported but year-on-year declines for critical sub-contractors are not analysed. Evaluate the causes and implications of this downward trend.
The narrative on supplier proximity is descriptive; link this detail explicitly to environmental or social performance within the triple-bottom-line framework.
The IWAY standard is reproduced in full. Consider summarising key requirements in the main text and moving lengthy excerpts to an appendix for concision.
Claims about rising supplier costs are made without reference to specific data or case studies. Integrate quantitative or expert evidence to substantiate this impact.
The research question is clearly stated, explicit and focused, mapping directly to the triple-bottom-line dimensions that structure the subsequent analysis.
The introduction contains extensive historical detail (founding date, valuation) that is descriptive rather than directly linked to the research question. Trim background to maintain focus.
Industry COâ figures are cited but not connected to IKEAâs sustainability initiatives. Explicitly tie these statistics to the companyâs goals to strengthen cohesion.
The STEEPLE factors are listed without explicitly linking them back to the research question. Draw direct connections between each external factor and triple-bottom-line outcomes.
Carrollâs Pyramid diagram is well integrated, clearly captioned and enhances the readerâs understanding of CSR layers.
The text accepts philanthropic responsibility as least important without critique. A more sophisticated evaluation would question or contextualise this normative ranking.
The sentence âAdditionally, research showsâ is incomplete. Provide the full finding or remove the fragment to maintain readability.
Figure 8 effectively shows workload distribution but lacks a descriptive title on the chart itself. Include titles on visuals for standalone clarity.
Data collection relies heavily on IKEAâs own publications. Consider incorporating independent audits or academic sources to triangulate and validate self-reported figures.
Job displacement is quantified but lacks discussion of strategic trade-offs or alternative workforce solutions. Explore how IKEA could mitigate these negative impacts.
The 2024 employee satisfaction survey is cited but not linked to financial or operational outcomes. Discuss how declining engagement might affect performance metrics.
The ex-employee quote in Weaknesses lacks context and evaluation of source reliability. Critically assess anecdotal evidence before integrating into strategic analysis.
The bar chart clearly illustrates workforce changes; however, the axis label should specify âEmployees (thousands)â to clarify units.
The Venn diagram presents reasons for resistance but the narrative does not interpret the intersections. Explain how overlapping factors compound change resistance.
The overwork statistic is presented without evaluating survey methodology or response bias. Address potential limitations to strengthen the credibility of this claim.
